PROJECT 01

MLOps Workshop

Infrastructure as Code Pipeline on Azure DevOps

Azure DevOpsTerraformIaCCI/CDMLOps

Overview

End-to-end setup of an Infrastructure as Code pipeline deployed on Azure DevOps. The goal was to automate cloud resource provisioning using Terraform, enabling repeatable, version-controlled deployments — a foundational pattern in modern MLOps workflows.

What Was Built

  • Designed and deployed a fully automated CI/CD pipeline on Azure DevOps for infrastructure provisioning.
  • Used Terraform to define cloud resources declaratively, ensuring consistency across environments.
  • Integrated pipeline triggers to automatically apply infrastructure changes on code commits.
  • Validated with multi-stage deployments (dev → staging → production) with approval gates.
PROJECT 02

Amazon Bedrock

Generative AI Integration on AWS

Amazon BedrockAWSGenerative AIFoundation ModelsAPI

Overview

Exploration of Amazon Bedrock — AWS's fully managed service for building generative AI applications using foundation models. Focus was on connecting, configuring, and invoking large language models through the Bedrock API, creating a foundation for production-ready AI features within AWS-native infrastructure.

What Was Explored

  • Provisioned and configured Amazon Bedrock access within an AWS environment.
  • Tested multiple foundation models (Claude, Titan) through the Bedrock playground and API.
  • Explored prompt engineering patterns optimized for foundation model inference.
  • Evaluated response quality and latency characteristics across model families.
PROJECT 03

N8N: Gym Booking Agent

Automated Appointment Scheduling with AI

n8nGoogle Gemini 2.0GmailGoogle CalendarAgentic AI

Overview

A fully automated Gym Appointment Booking Agent built using n8n. Transforms natural-language chat messages into confirmed calendar events without any manual intervention — showcasing how agentic AI and workflow automation can eliminate repetitive scheduling overhead.

Key Features

  • AI-Powered Intent Recognition: Google Gemini 2.0 Flash interprets natural language, extracting date and time from diverse phrasing.
  • Automated Scheduling: Google Calendar Tool checks availability and creates 30-minute appointment slots automatically.
  • Instant Confirmation: Gmail triggered to send personalized confirmation emails immediately after booking.
  • Conversational Memory: Simple Memory retains chat context for a more human-like experience.
PROJECT 04

N8N: Agentic RAG Workflow

Document Upload & AI Chat Assistant

n8nOpenAIRAGVector StorePDF ProcessingAI Agent

Overview

An Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation workflow using n8n — enabling document upload, semantic vector storage, and an intelligent AI chat assistant that retrieves and synthesizes information from uploaded PDFs. Applicable to enterprise knowledge bases, customer support, and document query systems.

What Was Built

  1. Set up a custom n8n workflow for document upload and processing.
  2. Extracted PDF text using the Default Data Loader.
  3. Generated embeddings with OpenAI Embeddings, stored in a Simple Vector Store for semantic search.
  4. Configured a real-time chat interface using the Chat Trigger node.
  5. Connected AI Agent, OpenAI Chat Model, Simple Memory, and Vector Store for contextual responses.
  6. Activated and tested the complete AI chat system through n8n's chat UI.
PROJECT 05

AI-Powered IT Support

Multi-Agent Intake Orchestrator

Multi-Agent AIWorkflow AutomationLLMWeb SearchIT Operations

Overview

An automated IT Support Agent that streamlines incident reporting and data collection. Using a multi-agent architecture, the system autonomously triages user inquiries, identifies data gaps, and executes intelligent follow-up protocols to ensure every support request is comprehensive and actionable.

Technical Highlights

  • Orchestration & Logic: Multi-stage workflow with conditional nodes and triage agents routing requests based on real-time intent analysis.
  • Dynamic Information Retrieval: Helper agents detect missing parameters (device types, error codes) and prompt for precise details.
  • Advanced Reasoning: LLMs with web-search capabilities for diagnostic accuracy on complex issues.
  • Full-Cycle Automation: Front-end intake layer feeding into automated ticketing and multi-agent resolution systems.

Impact

Successfully managed end-to-end troubleshooting for Wi-Fi connectivity issues in live testing, demonstrating context retention and resolution without human intervention.

PROJECT 06

MiniAmazon

Full-Stack E-Commerce Demo Store

HTMLCSSJavaScriptFrontendE-Commerce
⬡ Live Demo →

Overview

A fully functional e-commerce demo store built from scratch using vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — no frameworks, no dependencies. Demonstrates core front-end engineering skills including dynamic cart management, checkout flow, and responsive UI design.

Features

  • Product catalog with browsable item listings and dynamic rendering via JavaScript.
  • Shopping cart with real-time item count and running total calculation.
  • Checkout flow with order summary and Place Order confirmation step.
  • Clean, responsive layout styled entirely with custom CSS — no UI library required.
PROJECT 07

Agentic Coding Assistant

AI-Powered Code Generation Agent

StreamlitLLMAgentic AICode GenerationPython
⬡ Live Demo →

Overview

An agentic coding assistant that goes beyond simple code generation. The agent autonomously plans, reasons, and iterates — breaking down coding tasks into steps, writing code, reflecting on output, and refining results to deliver higher-quality solutions.

Key Capabilities

  • Agentic task planning: decomposes complex coding requests into sub-tasks and executes sequentially.
  • Multi-turn reasoning: maintains context across steps, allowing the agent to debug and improve its own code.
  • Broad language support: Python, JavaScript, SQL, and general programming logic tasks.
  • Explainability: surfaces intermediate reasoning steps, making the agent's decisions transparent.
PROJECT 08

AI Research Crew

Multi-Agent Research Orchestration

CrewAIMulti-AgentStreamlitLLMWeb SearchPython
⬡ Live Demo →

Overview

A multi-agent system that orchestrates a team of specialized AI agents to conduct in-depth research on any topic. The system delegates tasks across a Researcher, Analyst, and Writer agent — each with a defined role, toolset, and responsibility.

Agent Roles

  • Researcher Agent: autonomously searches the web for current, relevant information using live search tools.
  • Analyst Agent: synthesizes gathered data, identifying key insights, trends, and supporting evidence.
  • Writer Agent: produces a well-structured, coherent research report from the analyzed findings.
PROJECT 09

Veristack RAG

Intelligent Document Q&A System

RAGVector DatabaseOpenAIStreamlitPythonLangChain
⬡ Live Demo →

Overview

A production-grade Retrieval-Augmented Generation application — users upload documents and ask natural language questions, receiving accurate answers sourced directly from their own content. Every response is anchored to verifiable retrieved context, minimizing hallucination.

How It Works

  1. Upload documents (PDF, text) through the Streamlit interface.
  2. Documents are chunked, embedded with OpenAI Embeddings, and stored in a vector database.
  3. Questions trigger semantic retrieval of the most relevant document chunks.
  4. LLM generates a precise, context-grounded answer — citing only uploaded content.

Use Cases

  • Legal document review and contract analysis.
  • Enterprise knowledge management and internal Q&A.
  • Academic research assistance with source attribution.
